artPrompt: The unsettling instant a hulking sludge-monster looming in the wide frame is split by a glowing arithmetic seam of sickly bioluminescence, its three luminous eyes flaring as half of its bulk fragments into murky green motes drifting upward. Rusted pipes and bubbling muck frame the lower dark. One bruised storm-light rakes its dividing form, rust-red gleaming, rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Divide the Monster by Its Eyes
text:
Take the largest creature at the table and divide its power by the number of eyes it has. The catfish has three eyes. Everything has three eyes now, you just checked, blink twice if you understand. The quotient is left as an exercise for the reader.
Whatever remains deals the remainder damage to the remainder of the table. There is no remainder. There is only more eyes.
flavorText: Carry the one. The one carries you.
